deck.title: Project Name
deck.tagline: Four narratives. One story. Select a topic to explore.

pages.title: Topic One Title
pages.subtitle: Short supporting subtitle for this narrative.
pages.banner: Optional banner paragraph. Appears at the top of the screen as a highlighted statement about this topic.
pages.callout: One bold closing line that crystallizes the 'so what' of this topic for a senior audience.

cards.title: Card One
cards.body: Describe what happened, how it was achieved, or what this metric represents in plain language.

cards.title: Card Two
cards.body: Second supporting point. Keep it factual and punchy — one insight per card.

cards.title: Card Three
cards.body: Third point. End with the impact or implication — not just the activity.

pages.title: Topic Two Title
pages.subtitle: Challenge and resolution framing for this section.
pages.callout: One bold closing statement summarizing the transformation this section describes.

cards.title: Challenge One
cards.before: Describe the original problem, friction, or limitation that existed.
cards.after: Describe the solution, improvement, or new approach that resolved it.

cards.title: Challenge Two
cards.before: Another pain point or obstacle the team faced early on.
cards.after: How the team addressed it — process change, tool adoption, or new standard.

cards.title: Challenge Three
cards.before: A compliance, legal, or organizational hurdle.
cards.after: The framework or approval process established to clear it.

cards.title: Challenge Four
cards.before: A skills gap or team readiness issue.
cards.after: The enablement approach — training, hackathon, pairing, etc.

pages.title: Phased Roadmap
pages.subtitle: Phased rollout from pilot to scale.
pages.callout: Each phase builds on validated outcomes from the last. No phase starts until the prior one meets its exit criteria.

cards.title: Milestone 1A
cards.body: Description of this milestone and what it achieves.

cards.title: Milestone 1B
cards.body: Description of this milestone and what it achieves.

cards.title: Milestone 1C
cards.body: Description of this milestone and what it achieves.

cards.title: Milestone 2A
cards.body: Description of this milestone and what it achieves.

cards.title: Milestone 2B
cards.body: Description of this milestone and what it achieves.

cards.title: Milestone 3A
cards.body: Description of this milestone and what it achieves.

cards.title: Milestone 3B
cards.body: Description of this milestone and what it achieves.

cards.title: Milestone 4A
cards.body: Description of this milestone and what it achieves.

cards.title: Milestone 4B
cards.body: Description of this milestone and what it achieves.

pages.title: Process / Sprint Cycle
pages.subtitle: Human checkpoints at every stage of delivery.
pages.callout: The modified process cycle includes human-in-the-loop checkpoints and rapid iteration aligned to best practices.

pages.title: Outcomes & Results
pages.subtitle: What the numbers say about the work.
pages.callout: The playbook is proven. The question is no longer whether — it's how fast we scale.

cards.title: What We Proved
cards.body: Summarize the core proof point this project established.

cards.title: What Comes Next
cards.body: Describe the logical next step or investment case that follows.

pages.title: Who This Helps
pages.subtitle: Every role benefits differently.
pages.callout: Adoption succeeds when every stakeholder sees their own win in the story.

cards.title: Role A
cards.pain: Describe the pain point or frustration this role faces today.
cards.gain: Describe how the solution addresses their specific needs.

cards.title: Role B
cards.pain: Describe the pain point or frustration this role faces today.
cards.gain: Describe how the solution addresses their specific needs.

cards.title: Role C
cards.pain: Describe the pain point or frustration this role faces today.
cards.gain: Describe how the solution addresses their specific needs.

cards.title: Role D
cards.pain: Describe the pain point or frustration this role faces today.
cards.gain: Describe how the solution addresses their specific needs.

pages.title: Comparison Matrix
pages.subtitle: Cross-option evaluation for your context.
pages.callout: No single option wins everywhere. The right answer depends on your specific context and constraints.

pages.title: Framework Overview
pages.subtitle: Three pillars that support the approach.
pages.callout: The framework isn't overhead — it's what enables speed without sacrificing quality.
